The space between the reactor core and 6Li-D converter
was occupied by a large heavy water tank and Bi
The converter consisted of two equal pieces of 6LiD
(75 g, enriched to 95.5% in 6Li), canned in Al cases
with the dimensions 10 x 10 x 1 cm.
The activation samples were sandwiched between the two
converters, which were placed in a thermal neutron
flux of 1.5E+09 n/cm2sec on the front surface of the
The fast neutron flux obtained with 27Al(n,alpha) was
The irradiation room is about 2.4 m x 2.4 m x 2.4 m.
The energy of the 14 MeV neutrons were measured to be
14.05+/-0.07 MeV by activity ratio of irradiated Zr
The energy spectrum was measured by 13 different
activation cross sections, and unfolding with a
computer the NEUPAC code (see Table II of Nucl.Sci.
Eng.106(1990)332 for the input reaction rates and
In parallel with the spectrum measurement, transport
calculations by the ANISN and MCNP codes were also
Neutron spectrum obtained by the NEUPAC code
The activation foils were 12.7 mm in diameter and 0.1
The purity in each foil was more than 99%.
The data were corrected for activation by neutrons
of energy below 12 MeV and for some other factors.
